ACH-01.score_rationale: No evidence was found regarding SecuraOps' management of cybersecurity risks related to its own third-party suppliers or subcontractors.
gaps: No documented process for identifying or assessing third-party suppliers
No evidence of risk management activities (e.g., due diligence, monitoring, or reviews) for subcontractors
No mention of contractual security requirements for suppliers
ACH-01.recommendation: Implement a Third-Party Risk Management (TPRM) program to identify, assess, and monitor cybersecurity risks associated with SecuraOps' suppliers and subcontractors.

ACH-02.score_rationale: No evidence of explicit security clauses in contracts with SecuraOps' own subcontractors or third-party suppliers was found in the provided graph evidence.
gaps: No explicit security clauses in contracts with subcontractors
No documented right-to-audit or security review requirements for subcontractors
No evidence of contractual obligations related to security for Tier-N suppliers
ACH-02.recommendation: Include explicit security clauses, right-to-audit provisions, and security review requirements in all contracts with subcontractors and third-party suppliers.

DATA-01.score_rationale: Evidence shows TLS 1.3 is used for communications and encryption, but lacks confirmation of Perfect Forward Secrecy (PFS) activation and HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) implementation.

evidence.detail: Used for communications and encryption
Encryption and Key Management Policy (potential reference for TLS configuration)

gaps: No evidence of Perfect Forward Secrecy (PFS) activation
No evidence of HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) implementation
No technical details confirming enforcement of TLS 1.3 as minimum version
DATA-01.recommendation: Provide documented evidence of PFS activation, HSTS implementation, and explicit confirmation of TLS 1.3 as the minimum enforced version.

DATA-02.score_rationale: Evidence shows encryption at rest is implemented (AES-256, BitLocker, WORM) and key management (Azure Key Vault), but scope (e.g., production-only or all data) is not explicitly defined.

evidence.detail: use: Encryption at rest
use: Workstation Encryption
use: encrypted log archiving
use: key management

gaps: Scope of encryption at rest (all data, production-only, or partial) not specified
DATA-02.recommendation: Document the explicit scope of encryption at rest (e.g., all data or production-only) to fully meet the control requirements.

DATA-03.score_rationale: No technical evidence of SPF, DKIM, or DMARC configuration for SecuraOps' email domains was found in the provided graph evidence.
gaps: Missing SPF record for SecuraOps' email domain
Missing DKIM configuration for SecuraOps' email domain
Missing DMARC policy for SecuraOps' email domain
No evidence of alignment parameters or reporting addresses
DATA-03.recommendation: Publish explicit SPF, DKIM, and DMARC records for SecuraOps' email domains in DNS, including alignment parameters and reporting addresses.

ENDP-01.score_rationale: Evidence shows the presence of an EDR solution for workstations and incident detection, but lacks details on centralized management, firewall activation, or coverage for servers.

evidence.detail: Incident detection and response
Workstation Protection

gaps: No evidence of centralized management of protections
No mention of firewall activation on endpoints
No evidence of EDR or antivirus coverage for servers
ENDP-01.recommendation: Document and provide evidence of centralized management, firewall activation on all endpoints, and protection coverage for servers.

ENDP-02.score_rationale: No evidence related to full-disk encryption (FDE) on workstations, deployment scope, or key management was found in the provided graph.
gaps: No evidence of FDE activation on workstations
No evidence of deployment scope (all workstations or partial)
No evidence of key recovery management
ENDP-02.recommendation: Provide documented evidence of FDE implementation on workstations, including deployment scope and key recovery procedures.

ENDP-03.score_rationale: Partial evidence of hardening practices exists (CISO validation for exposed components), but no comprehensive baseline, standards, or scope coverage is documented.

evidence.detail: Validation by the CISO for any exposed component during the deployment phase

gaps: No documented baseline of secure configuration
No reference to hardening standards (CIS, vendor baselines)
No evidence of admin rights restriction, device control, or application allowlisting/denylisting
No defined scope (workstations, servers, or all systems)
ENDP-03.recommendation: Document a hardening baseline aligned with industry standards (e.g., CIS) and define scope, admin rights restrictions, and device/application controls.

GOV-01.score_rationale: Evidence shows multiple security-related policies with identified owners and versioning, but no explicit Information Security Policy (ISP) document or documented review cycle is identified.

evidence.detail: Data Classification Policy | owner: Data Protection Officer (DPO) | version: 1.6 | date: October 2025
Encryption and Key Management Policy | owner: Crypto Officer
Approver role identified for governance bodies

gaps: No explicit Information Security Policy (ISP) document identified
No documented review cycle or frequency for policy updates
GOV-01.recommendation: Develop and formally document an overarching Information Security Policy (ISP) with a defined review cycle (e.g., annual review) and ensure it is approved by the CISO or IT Director.

GOV-02.score_rationale: Evidence shows a designated CISO role with formal responsibilities and hierarchical linkage, but lacks documented review cycle or explicit validation of responsibilities.

evidence.detail: Acronyme: CISO | role: Approver | name: CISO
title: Chief Information Security Officer (CISO)
role: Monthly reporting | name: Security Committee (implied CISO reporting line)

gaps: No explicit documentation of CISO's responsibilities scope
No evidence of formal review cycle for CISO's role effectiveness
GOV-02.recommendation: Document the CISO's responsibilities in a formal job description and establish a periodic review process for role effectiveness.

GOV-03.score_rationale: Evidence shows structured risk identification and mitigation protocols, but lacks formal documentation of a risk analysis process, frequency, methodology, or a risk register.

evidence.value: Vulnerabilities identified in developed or integrated software
evidence.detail: Risk levels: low, moderate, high, critical
General category of risks identified

evidence.value: CVSS v3.1, MITRE DeTT&CK, PDCA, TLS 1.3, IPsec, Failover plans, SHA-512
evidence.detail: Protocols mitigating identified risks

gaps: No formal risk analysis process documented
No evidence of frequency of risk analysis
No defined methodology for risk analysis
No risk register identified
GOV-03.recommendation: Document a formal risk analysis process including methodology, frequency, and maintain a risk register.

IAM-01.score_rationale: No valid evidence of MFA implementation for internal users (administrators or standard users) was found in the provided graph data.
gaps: No documented MFA policy or implementation for internal users
No evidence of MFA coverage scope (admin-only, all users, etc.)
No evidence of MFA method robustness (e.g., factors used)
IAM-01.recommendation: Implement and document MFA for all internal users, including administrators, with strong authentication factors (e.g., TOTP, hardware tokens) and define the coverage scope explicitly.

IAM-02.score_rationale: Evidence of a Joiner-Mover-Leaver (JML) process exists but lacks completeness, particularly in automation and rapidity of deprovisioning during leaver events.

evidence.detail: Defines Joiner-Mover-Leaver (JML) process for internal employees.
Linked to HR workflow for provisioning access during joiner phase.
Manual deprovisioning step triggered by HR exit notification.
Used for identity management but lacks evidence of automation for provisioning/deprovisioning.

gaps: No evidence of automated provisioning/deprovisioning for identities
No documented timeline for rapid deactivation of accounts during leaver events
Missing review cycle or validation step for JML process effectiveness
IAM-02.recommendation: Implement automated provisioning/deprovisioning tools and define a maximum timeline (e.g., 24 hours) for account deactivation during leaver events.

IAM-03.score_rationale: Evidence of a PAM solution and session recording exists, but scope, review cycles, and emergency access mechanisms are not fully documented or verified.

evidence.detail: Implementation of CyberArk for privileged access management
Session recording enabled for administrative access

gaps: No documented review cycle for privileged access
No evidence of a break-glass (emergency access) mechanism
Scope of PAM solution not fully defined (e.g., coverage of all critical systems)
IAM-03.recommendation: Document and implement a break-glass procedure and establish a regular review cycle for privileged access logs and accounts.
